compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P2 and P4 are currently devoid of visible foliage or stems. The yellow pot (P1) contains soil but no visible specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: Single leaf remaining; stem appears thin and potentially stressed.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of crushed eggshells in P3; white paper/receipt debris observed in T-5 to T-1; small electronic component (IC/chip) observed in T-4 and T-3.
biome_observations.anomalies: The presence of non-botanical debris (eggshells, electronics, paper) suggests an experimental or non-standard horticultural environment.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The Pothos (P3) has remained largely static with a single leaf. The other pots have shown no growth or recovery over the 5-day period. The removal of the electronic component and paper debris indicates external human intervention.

visual_health_inference.P3: Critical/Stagnant. The single leaf is the only remaining photosynthetic tissue.
visual_health_inference.P1_P2_P4: Deceased/Systemic Loss. No viable plant material detected.

anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in P3 soil.
Transient presence of electronic component (T-4, T-3).
Transient presence of paper receipt (T-5 to T-1).
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of severe decline. Only one specimen (P3) retains a single leaf, while P1, P2, and P4 are effectively empty. The environment is cluttered with non-organic debris, suggesting a lack of proper maintenance or an experimental setup that has failed to support plant life.
